title: pereskiopsis diguetii subsp. spathulata
intro: Pereskiopsis diguetii subsp. spathulata, also known as the Cholla Bajada, is a cactus species native to Mexico and California, characterized by its flat, blue-green stems and sharp spines. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Pereskiopsis diguetii subsp. spathulata include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various ailments, including colds, fever, and skin irritations.
